title: hieracium lamprophyllum subsp. murorramondii
intro: Hieracium lamprophyllum subsp. murorramondii, also known as the lesser hawk's beard, is a type of flowering plant in the dandelion family (Asteraceae) native to the Iberian Peninsula. Traditionally, the plant has been used for its medicinal properties, including anti-inflammatory and antioxidant effects, which can help alleviate symptoms of digestive issues and respiratory problems. The health benefits associated with Hieracium lamprophyllum subsp. murorramondii include its ability to soothe digestive issues, reduce inflammation, and boost the immune system. In herbal preparations, the plant is often used in teas, infusions, and tinctures to harness its medicinal properties, although more research is needed to fully understand its potential benefits and uses.
